caption.text: Do you know why your onboarding doesn’t work?
It’s not because it’s badly explained,
too short or too simple.
In most cases, it’s because there’s no aha moment.
So today I’m going to explain what a real aha moment is.
A lot of SaaS still see onboarding as a sign-up,
a setup or a mini tutorial, and that’s it.
But in reality,
onboarding goes from sign-up until the user is independent.
And that’s where the aha moment
matters. Because instead of showing a tutorial or just a checklist,
you’re going to guide the user to take one key action
that will show them the value of the SaaS
immediately.
In psychology, we compare aha moments to the Eureka effect.
It’s the moment when the user says to themselves,
“I get why I need to use this tool.”
For example, an online booking tool like Calendly.
When you sign up for this tool,
you might think the aha moment is setting up your schedule,
your slots, your time blocks.
But that’s not really it. Here,
the aha moment is when you generate your link and share it.
You open the link and you see your availability,
a quick booking with no need for long email exchanges.
And then you say to yourself,
“Okay, I’m bookable,
accessible, and I’ve got nothing left to manage.” That moment isn’t about the tool,
it’s about what the tool lets you become.
Visible, accessible,
reachable. And that’s exactly
a successful aha moment.
It’s not “I understood all the features,”
but “I immediately see how this changes my real life.”
That’s why the aha moment is often misunderstood.
We look for it in the features,
when in fact it’s in the product’s main promise:
made visible as fast as possible.
